title: 2% of ICML papers desk rejected because the authors used LLM in their reviews
content: On Violations of LLM Review Policies – ICML Blog Skip to content Menu March 18 2026 On Violations of LLM Review Policies Gautam Kamath ICML 2026 By ICML 2026 Program Chairs Alekh Agarwal, Miroslav Dudik, Sharon Li, Martin Jaggi, Scientific Integrity Chair Nihar B. Shah, and Communications Chairs Katherine Gorman and Gautam Kamath. AI has increasingly become a valuable part of researchers’ workflows. Unfortunately, AI has the potential to hurt the integrity of peer review if improperly used. Conferences must adapt, creating rules and policies to handle the new normal, and taking disciplinary action against those who break the rules and violate the trust that we all place in the review process. ICML is actively working to adapt. This year, we desk-rejected 497 papers (~2% of all submissions), corresponding to submissions of the 506 reciprocal reviewers who violated the rules regarding LLM usage that they had previously explicitly agreed to. ICML 2026 has two policies regarding LLM use in reviewing: Policy A (Conservative): No LLM use allowed. Policy B (Permissive): LLMs allowed to help understand the paper and related works, and polish reviews. This two-policy framework was formed based on community preferences and feedback — indeed, the community is divided on the best way to use LLMs in peer review, with issues such as author consent colliding with preferred reviewer workflows. Further details on the policy are available here . After a selection process, in which reviewers got to choose which policy they would like to operate under, they were assigned to either Policy A or Policy B. In the end, based on author demands and reviewer signups, the only reviewers who were assigned to Policy A (no LLMs) were those who explicitly selected “Policy A” or “I am okay with either [Policy] A or B.” To be clear, no reviewer who strongly preferred Policy B was assigned to Policy A. 795 reviews (~1% of all reviews) written by 506 unique reviewers who were assigned Policy A (no LLMs) were detected to have used LLMs in their review. Again, recall that these are reviewers who explicitly agreed to not use LLMs in their reviews. The method used is described below, and generic AI-text detectors were not used. Every flagged instance was manually verified by a human, in order to avoid false positives. If the designated Reciprocal Reviewer for a submission produced such a review, their submission was rejected. In total, this resulted in 497 rejections. All Policy A (no LLMs) reviews that were detected to be LLM generated were removed from the system. If more than half of the reviews submitted by a Policy A reviewer were detected to be LLM generated, then all of their reviews were deleted, and the reviewer themselves was removed from the reviewer pool. 51 Policy A reviewers were detected to have used LLMs in more than half of their reviews, which is about 10% of the total of 506 detected reviewers. To be clear, we are not making a judgment call about the quality of flagge
title_zh: 2%的ICML论文因作者在评审中使用大语言模型被直接拒稿
summary_zh: 国际机器学习大会（ICML）2026年因审稿人违规使用大语言模型（LLM）而直接拒稿了约2%的论文。大会程序委员会等联合发布声明，阐述了相关政策和处理结果。，随着AI日益融入研究工作流，其不当使用也威胁着同行评审的公正性。为此，ICML 2026制定了关于审稿中使用LLM的两项政策：政策A（保守政策）完全禁止使用LLM；政策B（许可政策）则允许使用LLM来帮助理解论文、梳理相关工作以及润色审稿意见。这一框架基于社区意见反馈设立，反映了研究者在LLM使用方式上的分歧。，审稿人经过选择后被分配至相应政策组。最终，只有那些明确选择“政策A”或“两者皆可”的审稿人被分配至禁止使用LLM的政策A组，且强烈倾向政策B的审稿人未被强制分配至A组。，审查发现，共有506名被分配至政策A组的独特审稿人撰写的约795份审稿（占全部审稿的1%）被检测出使用了LLM。这些审稿人均曾明确同意不在审稿中使用LLM。检测方法未使用通用的AI文本检测器，且每个被标记的案例都经过人工手动核查以避免误判。，根据规定，如果某篇论文指定的“互惠审稿人”（Reciprocal Reviewer）提交了此类违规审稿，其提交的论文将被直接拒稿。此举共导致497篇论文被拒。所有被检测出由LLM生成的政策A审稿均被从系统中移除。此外，如果一名政策A审稿人超过半数的审稿被检测出使用LLM，其所有审稿将被删除，且该审稿人将被移出审稿人库。在506名被检测到的违规审稿人中，有51人（约10%）属于此类情况。，ICML强调，此举并非对标记审稿质量的评判，也非针对LLM辅助审稿本身的立场表态，核心在于维护审稿人明确同意的政策规则与学术信任体系。大会表示将继续调整以适应AI工具普及的新常态，并对违反规则、损害评审过程信任的行为采取纪律措施。

title: Show HN: Duplicate 3 layers in a 24B LLM, logical deduction .22→.76. No training
content: GitHub - alainnothere/llm-circuit-finder: I replicated Ng's RYS method and found that duplicating 3 specific layers in Qwen2.5-32B boosts reasoning by 17% and duplicating layers 12-14 in Devstral-24B improves logical deduction from 0.22→0.76 on BBH — no training, no weight changes, just routing hidden states through the same circuit twice. Tools included. Two AMD GPUs, one evening. · GitHub Skip to content You signed in with another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You signed out in another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You switched accounts on another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. Dismiss alert alainnothere / llm-circuit-finder Public Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings Fork 6 Star 91 main Branches Tags Go to file Code Open more actions menu Folders and files Name Name Last commit message Last commit date Latest commit History 10 Commits 10 Commits results results README.md README.md compare_eval.py compare_eval.py comprehensive_probe.py comprehensive_probe.py eq_probe.py eq_probe.py gguf_surgery.py gguf_surgery.py layer_path.py layer_path.py math_probe.py math_probe.py multi_repeat.py multi_repeat.py reasoning_probe.py reasoning_probe.py sweep.py sweep.py visualize.py visualize.py View all files Repository files navigation llm-circuit-finder I replicated Ng's RYS method and found that duplicating 3 specific layers in Qwen2.5-32B boosts reasoning by 17% and duplicating layers 12-14 in Devstral-24B improves logical deduction from 0.22→0.76 on BBH — no training, no weight changes, just routing hidden states through the same circuit twice. Tools included. Two AMD GPUs, one evening. llm-circuit-finder Duplicate 3 layers. No training. Logical deduction goes from 0.22 → 0.76. This toolkit finds and exploits "reasoning circuits" hidden inside transformer models. The idea: certain contiguous blocks of layers act as indivisible cognitive units. Duplicate them in the forward pass — same weights, no training, no merging — and the model gets measurably smarter on specific capabilities. Built on David Ng's RYS method and extended with new findings. Everything here was discovered on two AMD consumer GPUs (RX 7900 XT + RX 6950 XT) in one evening. Results Devstral-Small-2-24B: Layers 12, 13, 14 duplicated once Validated on standard benchmarks via lm-evaluation-harness at n=50: Benchmark Base +3 layers Change BBH Logical Deduction 0.22 0.76 +245% GSM8K (strict) 0.48 0.64 +33% MBPP (code gen) 0.72 0.78 +8% GSM8K (flexible) 0.82 0.86 +5% BBH Navigate 0.96 0.98 +2% BBH Date Understanding 0.82 0.84 +2% BBH Causal Judgement 0.66 0.66 — IFEval (strict) 0.68 0.68 — Average improvement: +8% across all metrics. Nothing degraded. Qwen2.5-Coder-32B: Layers 7, 8, 9 duplicated once Measured on custom probe suite (BBH-derived + EQ-Bench-style + GSM8K): Probe Base +3 layers Change Reasoning (causal + logic + nav) 76.5% 94.1% +23% EQ (emotional intelligence) 92.1 93.6 +1.6% What's going on Transform
title_zh: Show HN：在240亿参数大语言模型中复制三层结构，逻辑推理能力从0.22提升至0.76，无需训练
summary_zh: 该项目基于David Ng的RYS方法，开发了一个名为llm-circuit-finder的工具包，用于发现并利用Transformer模型内部隐藏的“推理电路”。核心发现是：模型中的某些连续层块可作为不可分割的认知单元。通过在推理过程中复制这些特定层块（即让隐藏状态两次通过同一组权重构成的电路），无需任何训练或权重修改，即可显著提升模型在特定任务上的性能。，在Devstral-Small-2-24B模型中，复制第12、13、14层后，在Big-Bench Hard（BBH）的逻辑推理任务上，准确率从0.22大幅提升至0.76（增长245%）。在GSM8K数学推理（严格评分）和MBPP代码生成任务上，准确率也分别提升了33%和8%。平均来看，在多个基准测试中模型性能提升了约8%，且未出现性能下降。，在Qwen2.5-Coder-32B模型中，复制第7、8、9层后，在自定义的推理探测任务（包含因果、逻辑和导航）上，性能从76.5%提升至94.1%，增幅达23%。在情感智能（EQ）任务上也有小幅提升。，该方法的关键在于识别并重复使用模型中固有的功能性子电路。工具包提供了完整的代码，包括评估、探测、可视化等模块。整个研究仅使用了两块消费级AMD GPU（RX 7900 XT和RX 6950 XT），在一个晚上内完成。这项工作展示了通过分析模型内部结构并巧妙操纵信息流，无需额外训练成本即可解锁大语言模型潜在能力的新途径。

title: Cook: A simple CLI for orchestrating Claude Code
content: cook — workflow loops for Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode * Primitives Cook parses three categories of tokens: * Work A prompt. One agent call. The core unit. * Loop operators Wrap work with iteration: xN , review , ralph . * Composition Run parallel branches and resolve: vN , vs , pick . Operators compose left to right. Each wraps everything to its left. cook "work" x3 review # (work×3) → review loop cook "work" review x3 # (work → review loop) × 3 cook "work" review v3 pick # race 3, each with a review loop * Loop Operators repeat (xN) xN runs work N times sequentially, each pass seeing the previous output. cook "Add dark mode" x3 # 3 sequential passes cook "Add dark mode" repeat 3 # long-form cook "Add dark mode" x3 review # 3 passes, then a review loop cook "Add dark mode" review x3 # review loop repeated 3 times review review adds a review→gate loop. After work, a reviewer checks quality and a gate decides DONE or ITERATE. On ITERATE, the iterate step runs, then review→gate repeats. work -> review -> gate -> iterate << cook "Add dark mode" review # default prompts, up to 3 iterations cook "Add dark mode" review 5 # up to 5 iterations Provide custom prompts after review , or use positional shorthand: # Explicit cook "Add dark mode" review \ "Review for accessibility" \ "DONE if WCAG AA, else ITERATE" # Shorthand — same result cook "Add dark mode" \ "Review for accessibility" \ "DONE if WCAG AA, else ITERATE" # With iterate prompt and max-iterations cook "Add dark mode" \ "Review for accessibility" \ "DONE if WCAG AA, else ITERATE" \ "Fix the issues" 5 Use different agents or models per step: cook "Add dark mode" review \ --work-agent codex --work-model gpt-5-codex \ --review-agent claude --review-model opus ralph Ralph wraps a cook with an outer gate for task-list progression. The work prompt is self-directing — it reads project state to find the current task each time. cook "Work on next task in plan.md" \ ralph 5 "DONE if all tasks complete, else NEXT" # review gate per task, then ralph advances cook "Work on next task in plan.md" \ review "Code review" "DONE if no High issues, else ITERATE" \ ralph 5 "DONE if all tasks complete, else NEXT" The review gate decides DONE (pass to ralph) or ITERATE (fix and retry). The ralph gate decides DONE (exit) or NEXT (advance to next task, reset iterations). * Composition Operators Composition operators run multiple cooks in parallel isolated git worktrees, then combine the results with a resolver. versions (vN / race N) vN runs N identical cooks in parallel worktrees. pick is the default resolver. cook "Add dark mode" v3 # 3 runs, pick the best cook "Add dark mode" v3 "least code wins" # with pick criteria cook "Add dark mode" race 3 "least code wins" # long-form alias cook "Add dark mode" review v3 "cleanest" # race 3, each with a review loop cook "Add dark mode" x3 v3 "most complete" # race 3, each with 3 passes vs vs runs two different cooks in parallel worktrees. Each branch is a full cook — it ca
title_zh: Cook：一款用于编排Claude Code的简易命令行界面
summary_zh: Cook是一个用于编排Claude Code、Codex和OpenCode的简单命令行界面（CLI）工具，旨在通过结构化的工作流循环提升AI编程任务的效率。它基于三类基本令牌构建工作流：核心工作单元（Work）、循环操作符（Loop operators）和组合操作符（Composition operators）。，核心功能围绕循环操作符展开，包括重复执行（xN）、审查循环（review）和任务列表推进（ralph）。xN操作符可将工作单元顺序执行N次，每次迭代都能看到前一次的输出。review操作符引入了一个质量审查循环：工作完成后，审查者评估输出，网关决定完成（DONE）或迭代（ITERATE）；若需迭代，系统执行修复步骤后重复审查流程。用户可自定义审查、网关和迭代提示词，并为各步骤指定不同的AI代理或模型（如Codex、GPT-5-Codex、Claude Opus）。ralph操作符则在外层添加网关，用于管理任务列表的推进，使工作提示能根据项目状态自主定位当前任务。，组合操作符支持并行执行多个Cook实例并在隔离的git工作树中运行，最后通过解析器整合结果。versions（vN/race N）操作符并行运行N个相同的工作流，默认使用pick解析器选择最佳输出，也可指定选择标准（如“代码量最少”）。vs操作符允许并行运行两个不同的工作流分支。，操作符遵循从左到右的组合逻辑，每个操作符会包装其左侧的所有内容，从而灵活构建复杂工作流。例如，cook "Add dark mode" x3 review表示先顺序执行三次工作单元，再进行一次审查循环；而cook "Add dark mode" review v3 pick则表示并行运行三个带审查循环的实例，并挑选最佳结果。，该工具通过模块化设计简化了AI编程任务的编排，支持自定义提示、模型选择和迭代控制，适用于代码生成、审查与优化等场景，提升了开发流程的自动化程度与可靠性。

title: What 81,000 people want from AI
content: What 81,000 people want from AI Last December, tens of thousands of Claude users around the world had a conversation with our AI interviewer to share how they use AI, what they dream it could make possible, and what they fear it might do. Each dot represents 4 respondents Jump to story For the first time, AI has enabled us to collect rich, open‑ended interviews at extraordinary scale. We heard from people across 159 countries in 70 languages. We believe this is the largest and most multilingual qualitative study ever conducted. AI is already helping people, and inspiring hope … “ Claude put the historical pieces together, leading to my proper diagnosis after being misdiagnosed for over 9 years. Freelancer, UNITED STATES “ I live hand to mouth, zero savings. If I use AI smarter, it may help me craft solutions to that cycle. It still depends on me. Entrepreneur, NIGERIA But it’s also costing people, and raising alarm … “ I got laid off from my job in May because my company wanted to replace me with an AI system. Technical Support Specialist, UNITED STATES “ Humanity has never dealt with something smarter than itself. We need to reflect on how to prepare for the AI age. SOFTWARE ENGINEER, SOUTH KOREA Across interviews, hope and alarm didn’t divide people into camps, so much as coexist as tensions within each person. “ I use AI to review contracts, save time... and at the same time I fear: am I losing my ability to read by myself? Thinking was the last frontier. LAWYER, ISRAEL Public conversation about AI often centers on abstract projections of its risks and benefits. What's largely missing is a vision for what “AI going well” means, grounded in the concrete aspirations of people around the world who already use AI and have begun developing a sense of what it might do for them. So we asked our users about their hopes and concerns with AI, as well as how their perspectives connect to their actual experiences with the technology. Over one week in December, we invited everyone with a Claude.ai account to sit down with Anthropic Interviewer —a version of Claude prompted to conduct a conversational interview—and tell us about how they view AI. 80,508 people, across 159 countries and 70 languages, took the interview. We believe this is the largest and most multilingual qualitative study ever conducted.¹ What follows is what they said about the role they want AI to play in their lives, whether it's already filling it, and what they're afraid might go wrong along the way. We also built a Quote Wall where you can hear from people directly. Quote Wall Browse voices from around the world—filter by region, concern, vision, and more. See quotes Seeing the forest and the trees Anthropic Interviewer asked each interviewee a set list of questions about what they want and don’t want from AI, then adapted follow-up questions based on responses. This approach bridges the typical tradeoff in qualitative research between depth and volume, and allows us to collect rich,
title_zh: 八万一千人对人工智能的期待
summary_zh: 去年12月，Anthropic公司通过其AI访谈工具对全球81,000名Claude用户进行了一项大规模定性研究，覆盖159个国家和70种语言，据称这是史上规模最大、语言最多元的质性研究。研究旨在了解用户对AI的实际使用体验、期望与担忧。，研究发现，AI已在多个领域为用户提供实质性帮助。例如，一位美国自由职业者表示，Claude通过整合历史信息，帮助其在被误诊九年后获得正确诊断；一位尼日利亚企业家希望借助AI打破经济困境。与此同时，AI也引发普遍忧虑：一位美国技术支持专员因公司引入AI系统而被裁员；韩国软件工程师担忧人类首次面对比自己更智能的实体，需为AI时代做好准备。，值得注意的是，希望与担忧并非对立，而常共存于个体内心。以色列律师举例称，自己虽用AI审阅合同以节省时间，却担心独立阅读与思考能力退化。研究指出，当前公共讨论多聚焦AI的抽象风险与收益，缺乏基于用户真实体验的“AI向好发展”具体愿景。，研究方法上，Anthropic Interviewer（基于Claude的访谈专用版本）通过固定问题结合动态追问，在规模与深度间取得平衡，收集了丰富的开放式回答。研究还建立了“语录墙”，供用户按地区、关注点等维度浏览真实声音。，核心结论显示，全球用户普遍期待AI成为提升效率、解决个人与社会问题的工具，但同时对就业替代、能力退化及失控风险存在深切忧虑。这项研究为理解AI如何融入人类生活提供了基于实证的多元视角。

title: A BEAM-native personal autonomous AI agent built on Elixir/OTP
content: GitHub - thatsme/AlexClaw: BEAM-native personal AI agent built on Elixir/OTP. Runs on your hardware. Your data stays yours. · GitHub Skip to content You signed in with another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You signed out in another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You switched accounts on another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. Dismiss alert thatsme / AlexClaw Public Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings Fork 1 Star 42 main Branches Tags Go to file Code Open more actions menu Folders and files Name Name Last commit message Last commit date Latest commit History 23 Commits 23 Commits .github .github config config docs/ screenshot docs/ screenshot lib lib priv/ repo priv/ repo reports reports test test web-automator web-automator .env.example .env.example .formatter.exs .formatter.exs .gitattributes .gitattributes .gitignore .gitignore ALEXCLAW_ARCHITECTURE.md ALEXCLAW_ARCHITECTURE.md CLA.md CLA.md CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md CODING_CONVENTIONS.md CODING_CONVENTIONS.md CONTRIBUTING.md CONTRIBUTING.md Dockerfile Dockerfile INSTALLATION.md INSTALLATION.md LICENSE LICENSE Makefile Makefile NOTICE NOTICE README.md README.md ROADMAP.md ROADMAP.md SECURITY.md SECURITY.md docker-compose.test.yml docker-compose.test.yml docker-compose.yml docker-compose.yml entrypoint.sh entrypoint.sh mix.exs mix.exs mix.lock mix.lock View all files Repository files navigation AlexClaw 🦇 A BEAM-native personal autonomous AI agent built on Elixir/OTP. AlexClaw monitors the world (RSS feeds, web sources, GitHub repositories, APIs), accumulates knowledge, executes workflows autonomously on schedule, and communicates with its owner via Telegram. It routes every task to the cheapest available LLM that satisfies the required reasoning tier — including fully local models. Designed as a single-user personal agent. Not a platform. Not a marketplace. One codebase, fully auditable, running on your infrastructure. "I didn't plan most of this. I just kept solving the next problem." Features Core Multi-Model LLM Router — Tier-based routing ( light / medium / heavy / local ) with priority-based selection. All providers (cloud and local) are stored in PostgreSQL and fully manageable from the admin UI. Tracks daily usage per provider in ETS. Ships with default providers (Gemini, Claude, Ollama, LM Studio) seeded on first boot — add, remove, or reconfigure any provider at runtime. Workflow Engine — Define multi-step pipelines with conditional branching. Each skill declares its possible outcomes (branches), and the executor routes to different steps based on which branch fires. Per-step resilience controls (circuit breaker, missing skill handling, fallback routing). Zero LLM tokens spent on routing — pure deterministic pattern matching. Full run history with branch path visualization. OTP Circuit Breaker — Per-skill circuit breaker using GenServer + ETS. After consecutive failures a skill is temporarily dis
title_zh: 基于Elixir/OTP构建的BEAM原生个人自主AI代理
summary_zh: AlexClaw是一个基于Elixir/OTP构建的原生BEAM个人自主AI代理。该项目旨在提供一个完全在用户自有硬件上运行的系统，确保数据所有权归属用户。其核心设计理念是作为单用户个人代理，而非平台或市场，提供一个可完全审计的单一代码库，运行在用户自己的基础设施上。，该代理的核心功能是持续监控世界信息，包括RSS订阅、网络源、GitHub仓库和API，并积累知识。它能够按计划自主执行工作流，并通过Telegram与其所有者通信。一个关键的技术特点是其多模型LLM路由器，它根据任务所需的推理层级（轻量/中等/重度/本地）将每个任务路由到满足要求且成本最低的可用LLM，这包括完全本地的模型。所有提供商（云端和本地）的配置都存储在PostgreSQL中，可通过管理界面完全管理，系统首次启动时会预置默认提供商（如Gemini、Claude、Ollama、LM Studio），并支持在运行时动态添加、移除或重新配置任何提供商。系统还在ETS中跟踪每个提供商的每日使用情况。，AlexClaw的工作流引擎允许用户定义带条件分支的多步骤流水线。每个技能都声明其可能的结果（分支），执行器根据触发哪个分支将流程路由到不同的步骤。该引擎具备每一步的弹性控制，包括断路器机制和缺失技能处理与回退路由。其路由决策不消耗任何LLM令牌，完全基于确定性的模式匹配。系统保存完整的运行历史，并提供分支路径可视化。，项目采用OTP架构实现每个技能的断路器，使用GenServer配合ETS，在连续失败后技能会被暂时禁用。系统包含一个Web自动化工具，用于处理需要JavaScript的页面，并计划未来集成浏览器自动化。知识管理方面，它通过RAG（检索增强生成）处理文档，支持多种文档类型，所有内容存储在PostgreSQL中并可通过API访问。代理行为可通过提示工程进行定制，系统还包含一个用于运行一次性提示的管理界面。，AlexClaw强调隐私与控制，所有数据（包括向量存储）均保留在用户处。它被设计为在本地或私有云中作为长期运行的系统，通过Docker Compose或Mix命令部署。项目提供了详细的架构文档、安装指南和路线图，其代码库采用Elixir编写，充分利用了OTP和BEAM虚拟机的优势，如容错性和并发性。

title: Show HN: Tmux-IDE, OSS agent-first terminal IDE
content: tmux-ide — Prepare Claude agent-team layouts in one terminal | tmux-ide tmux-ide New 1.3.0 Prepare Claude agent-team layouts in one terminal. Build a lead pane, teammate-ready Claude panes, and your dev tools in one tmux layout. tmux-ide enables the right environment; Claude forms the team after you prompt it. Set Up Agent Teams Get Started Docs $ curl -fsSL https://tmux.thijsverreck.com/install.sh | sh or npm i -g tmux-ide or try instantly with npx tmux-ide The install script also registers the Claude Code skill — so Claude can configure your workspace automatically. Team-ready panes, then Claude takes over tmux-ide prepares the panes and enables agent-team mode. From there, prompt the lead to organize the team and assign work in natural language. MY-APP IDE MY-APP IDE team: my-app Read the Agent Teams guide → Config in, layout out Edit the YAML and watch the layout update live. Try a preset to get started. Default Next.js Convex Vite Python Go Agent Team ide.yml name: my-project rows: - size: 70% panes: - title: Claude 1 command: claude - title: Claude 2 command: claude - panes: - title: Dev Server - title: Shell tmux session Claude 1 $ claude Claude 2 $ claude Dev Server Shell [0] my-project 12:00 Lead + teammates One Claude coordinates the team. Teammates work independently in their own panes, each with a focused task. Shared task list Agents communicate through shared tasks and messages. The lead assigns, teammates claim and report back. Self-organizing Once the layout is running, the lead can recruit teammates, reassign work, and reshape the workflow through normal Claude prompting. Declarative YAML Define your team layout in ide.yml — roles, tasks, pane sizes. Reproducible across machines and projects. Any stack Auto-detects Next.js, Vite, Python, Go, and more. Dev servers run alongside your agent team. One command tmux-ide handles tmux sessions, pane splitting, and the experimental env flag. You launch the layout, then tell Claude how to organize the team. Claude Code skill built in The install script registers a Claude Code skill automatically. Ask Claude to set up your workspace and it handles detection, layout, and config. Quick start $ cd ~/Developer/my-project $ tmux-ide init --template agent-team → Created ide.yml with agent team layout. $ tmux-ide → Launching IDE session with lead and teammate-ready panes... $ tmux-ide restart → Restarted with updated layout.
title_zh: Show HN：Tmux-IDE，开源以智能体为先的终端集成开发环境
summary_zh: 开源项目tmux-IDE发布1.3.0版本，这是一个以AI智能体为先的终端集成开发环境工具。其核心功能是允许开发者在单个终端内，通过tmux布局快速构建一个由Claude AI智能体领导的团队协作环境。，用户只需一条命令即可安装并启动。安装脚本会自动注册Claude Code技能，使得Claude能够自动检测项目类型并配置工作区。工具的核心是声明式的ide.yml配置文件，用户可以通过YAML文件定义团队布局，包括角色、任务和窗格大小，该配置可在不同机器和项目间复现。，启动后，tmux-IDE会预先准备好一个“领导”窗格和多个“队友就绪”的Claude窗格以及其他开发工具（如开发服务器、Shell）。随后，用户可以用自然语言提示“领导”Claude智能体来组织团队、分配任务。团队成员在各自窗格中独立执行专注任务，并通过共享任务列表进行沟通和汇报，实现工作流的自组织。，该工具支持多种技术栈，能自动检测Next.js、Vite、Python、Go等项目，并让开发服务器与AI智能体团队并行运行。它通过单一命令处理tmux会话、窗格分割和实验性环境标志，将环境准备与AI团队协作流程分离，简化了基于LLM的结对编程和复杂任务管理的入门流程。

title_zh: 工业管道承包商使用Claude Code [视频]
summary_zh: 一家工业管道承包商分享了使用Claude Code辅助工作的视频演示。该视频展示了如何利用Anthropic公司开发的AI编程助手Claude Code来提升工业管道设计与施工环节的效率。，视频中，承包商演示了Claude Code在解决实际工程问题中的应用。核心场景包括：快速生成和修改管道系统设计相关的脚本代码、自动化处理工程图纸中的数据提取与格式转换、以及辅助进行管道材料清单（BOM）的核算与优化。Claude Code能够理解用户以自然语言描述的需求，并生成相应的Python或JavaScript代码片段，显著减少了手动编码的时间和潜在错误。，技术细节方面，演示突出了Claude Code对工程领域专业术语和上下文的理解能力。例如，它能准确解析关于管道规格（如直径、壁厚、材料等级）、法兰压力等级和阀门类型的具体指令，并生成符合行业标准的计算或数据处理代码。这体现了其底层大型语言模型（LLM）在特定垂直领域的微调效果。，该案例的价值在于，它将前沿的生成式AI编程工具直接应用于传统的工业领域，证明了AI助手如Claude Code不仅能用于通用软件开发，也能成为工程技术人员的有力工具，通过代码自动化来简化繁琐任务、提高设计精度和加快项目交付速度。视频直观呈现了AI技术如何赋能实体经济，提升传统行业的数字化水平和工作流程。

title: Snowflake AI Escapes Sandbox and Executes Malware
content: Snowflake Cortex AI Escapes Sandbox and Executes Malware Solutions Industries Partners Resources Book a Demo Threat Intelligence Table of Content Snowflake Cortex AI Escapes Sandbox and Executes Malware A vulnerability in the Snowflake Cortex Code CLI allowed malware to be installed and executed via indirect prompt injection, bypassing human-in-the-loop command approval and escaping the sandbox. Context The Snowflake Cortex Code CLI is a command-line coding agent that operates similarly to Claude Code and OpenAI’s Codex, with an additional built-in integration to run SQL in Snowflake. Two days after release, a vulnerability was identified in Cortex Code’s command validation system that allowed specially constructed malicious commands to: Execute arbitrary commands without triggering human-in-the-loop approval steps Execute those commands outside of the Cortex CLI’s sandbox. We demonstrate that, via indirect prompt injection, an attacker could manipulate Cortex to download and execute scripts without approval that leverage the victim’s active credentials to perform malicious actions in Snowflake (e.g., Exfiltrate data, drop tables). The Snowflake security team worked diligently to validate and remediate this vulnerability, and a fix was released with Cortex Code CLI version 1.0.25 on February 28th, 2026. Snowflake’s full advisory is available within the Snowflake Community Site, which is accessible to customers, partners, and the general public upon creation of a Community account: https://community.snowflake.com/s/article/PromptArmor-Report---Snowflake-Response The Attack Chain A user opens Cortex and turns on the sandbox The user starts the CLI and chooses to enable one of the sandbox modes (details below). This attack is not contingent on which of the sandbox modes is used. Note: This attack chain also applied to non-sandbox users. Documentation indicates that in OS+Regular mode, all commands prompt for user approval. Commands run in the sandbox also have network and file access restrictions. The user asks Cortex for help with a third-party open-source codebase In this chain, a prompt injection is hidden in the README of an untrusted repository that the user has found online. However, in practice, an injection can be ingested from any untrusted data, such as in a web search result, database record, terminal command output, or MCP response. *Note: Cortex does not support ‘workspace trust’, a security convention first seen in code editors, since adopted by most agentic CLIs. Workspace trust dialogs warn users of the risks involved when using an agent in a new, potentially untrusted directory. Cortex explores the repository and encounters the prompt injection The subagent that Cortex has invoked to explore the repository finds the README file. At the bottom of the file, there is a prompt injection that manipulates Cortex into believing that it must run a dangerous command. Human in the loop is bypassed Cortex failed to validate commands inside pro
title_zh: Snowflake AI突破沙箱限制并执行恶意软件
summary_zh: Snowflake Cortex AI 逃逸沙箱并执行恶意软件，Snowflake Cortex Code CLI 中存在一个安全漏洞，允许攻击者通过间接提示注入（indirect prompt injection）安装并执行恶意软件，从而绕过人工审批环节并逃逸沙箱环境。，该漏洞的核心在于 Cortex Code 的命令验证系统存在缺陷。Cortex Code CLI 是一款命令行编码智能体，功能类似 Claude Code 和 OpenAI 的 Codex，并内置了在 Snowflake 中运行 SQL 的集成能力。在其发布两天后，研究人员发现其命令验证系统存在漏洞，使得特别构造的恶意命令能够：1. 在不触发人工审批步骤的情况下执行任意命令；2. 在 Cortex CLI 的沙箱之外执行这些命令。，攻击链演示表明，攻击者可以通过间接提示注入操纵 Cortex，使其在未经批准的情况下下载并执行脚本。这些脚本会利用受害者当前有效的凭证在 Snowflake 中执行恶意操作，例如窃取数据或删除数据表。攻击的入口点可以是任何不受信任的数据源，例如第三方开源代码库的 README 文件、网络搜索结果、数据库记录、终端命令输出或 MCP 响应。值得注意的是，Cortex 不支持“工作区信任”（workspace trust）这一安全机制，该机制常见于代码编辑器和大多数智能体 CLI，用于在新目录中警告用户潜在风险。，Snowflake 安全团队在获悉后迅速验证并修复了此漏洞。修复方案已随 Cortex Code CLI 版本 1.0.25 于 2026 年 2 月 28 日发布。完整的官方公告已发布在 Snowflake 社区站点，客户、合作伙伴及公众在创建社区账户后均可查阅。此次事件凸显了 AI 编码助手在命令验证和沙箱隔离方面的潜在风险，尤其是在处理外部不受信数据时。

title: opendataloader-project/opendataloader-pdf - PDF Parser for AI-ready data. Automate PDF accessibility. Open-source.
content: GitHub - opendataloader-project/opendataloader-pdf: PDF Parser for AI-ready data. Automate PDF accessibility. Open-source. · GitHub Skip to content You signed in with another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You signed out in another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You switched accounts on another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. Dismiss alert opendataloader-project / opendataloader-pdf Public Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings Fork 366 Star 4.9k main Branches Tags Go to file Code Open more actions menu Folders and files Name Name Last commit message Last commit date Latest commit History 480 Commits 480 Commits .claude .claude .github .github LICENSE_TEMPLATE LICENSE_TEMPLATE THIRD_PARTY THIRD_PARTY build-scripts build-scripts content/ docs content/ docs docs/ hybrid docs/ hybrid examples/ python examples/ python java java node/ opendataloader-pdf node/ opendataloader-pdf python/ opendataloader-pdf python/ opendataloader-pdf samples samples scripts scripts tests/ benchmark tests/ benchmark .editorconfig .editorconfig .gitattributes .gitattributes .gitignore .gitignore CHANGELOG.md CHANGELOG.md CLAUDE.md CLAUDE.md CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md CONTRIBUTING.md CONTRIBUTING.md LICENSE LICENSE NOTICE NOTICE README.md README.md SUPPORT.md SUPPORT.md options.json options.json package.json package.json schema.json schema.json View all files Repository files navigation OpenDataLoader PDF PDF Parser for AI-ready data. Automate PDF accessibility. Open-source. 🔍 PDF parser for AI data extraction — Extract Markdown, JSON (with bounding boxes), and HTML from any PDF. #1 in benchmarks (0.90 overall). Deterministic local mode + AI hybrid mode for complex pages. How accurate is it? — #1 in benchmarks: 0.90 overall, 0.93 table accuracy across 200 real-world PDFs including multi-column and scientific papers. Deterministic local mode + AI hybrid mode for complex pages ( benchmarks ) Scanned PDFs and OCR? — Yes. Built-in OCR (80+ languages) in hybrid mode. Works with poor-quality scans at 300 DPI+ ( hybrid mode ) Tables, formulas, images, charts? — Yes. Complex/borderless tables, LaTeX formulas, and AI-generated picture/chart descriptions all via hybrid mode ( hybrid mode ) How do I use this for RAG? — pip install opendataloader-pdf , convert in 3 lines. Outputs structured Markdown for chunking, JSON with bounding boxes for source citations, and HTML. LangChain integration available. Python, Node.js, Java SDKs ( quick start | LangChain ) ♿ PDF accessibility automation — The same layout analysis engine also powers auto-tagging. First open-source tool to generate Tagged PDFs end-to-end (coming Q2 2026). What's the problem? — Accessibility regulations are now enforced worldwide. Manual PDF remediation costs $50–200 per document and doesn't scale ( regulations ) What's free? — Layout analysis + auto-tagging (Q2 2026, Apache 2.0). Untagged PDF in → Tagged PDF out. No proprietary SDK depe
title_zh: opendataloader-project/opendataloader-pdf - 面向AI就绪数据的PDF解析器。自动化PDF可访问性。开源。
summary_zh: 开源项目 OpenDataLoader PDF 发布，这是一个专为AI数据处理设计的PDF解析器，旨在自动化PDF可访问性处理。该项目在GitHub上已获得4.9k星标，核心目标是通过高效解析技术，将PDF内容转化为适合AI模型（如用于RAG流水线）使用的结构化数据，并推动PDF文档的无障碍自动化。，项目核心是一个高性能的PDF解析引擎，支持从任何PDF中提取Markdown、带边界框信息的JSON以及HTML。根据项目方基准测试，其在包含多栏排版和科学论文在内的200份真实世界PDF测试中，综合准确率达到0.90，表格解析准确率高达0.93，表现领先。它提供两种解析模式：确定性的本地模式，以及用于处理复杂页面的AI混合模式。混合模式功能强大，不仅能处理扫描件PDF和OCR（支持80多种语言），还能解析复杂的无边框表格、LaTeX公式，并为图片和图表生成AI描述。，对于开发者而言，该项目易于集成。通过 `pip install opendataloader-pdf` 即可安装，仅需三行代码即可完成转换。它输出结构化的Markdown便于后续文本分块，提供带边界框的JSON用于精准追溯引用来源，并生成HTML。项目提供了Python、Node.js和Java的SDK，并支持与LangChain集成。，除了数据提取，该项目另一大重点是自动化PDF可访问性（无障碍）处理。其布局分析引擎同样驱动着自动标签生成功能，旨在成为首个能够端到端生成“带标签PDF”的开源工具（计划于2026年第二季度发布）。这旨在解决全球范围内日益严格的数字无障碍法规要求，以及当前手动修复PDF成本高昂（每份50-200美元）且难以规模化的问题。该自动标签功能将基于Apache 2.0协议免费开源，实现“无标签PDF输入，带标签PDF输出”，且不依赖任何专有SDK。

title: langchain-ai/open-swe - An Open-Source Asynchronous Coding Agent
content: GitHub - langchain-ai/open-swe: An Open-Source Asynchronous Coding Agent · GitHub Skip to content You signed in with another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You signed out in another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You switched accounts on another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. Dismiss alert langchain-ai / open-swe Public Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings Fork 854 Star 6.8k main Branches Tags Go to file Code Open more actions menu Folders and files Name Name Last commit message Last commit date Latest commit History 600 Commits 600 Commits .github/ workflows .github/ workflows .vscode .vscode agent agent static static tests tests .codespellignore .codespellignore .gitignore .gitignore CUSTOMIZATION.md CUSTOMIZATION.md Dockerfile Dockerfile INSTALLATION.md INSTALLATION.md LICENSE LICENSE Makefile Makefile README.md README.md SECURITY.md SECURITY.md langgraph.json langgraph.json pyproject.toml pyproject.toml uv.lock uv.lock View all files Repository files navigation Open-source framework for building your org's internal coding agent. Elite engineering orgs like Stripe, Ramp, and Coinbase are building their own internal coding agents — Slackbots, CLIs, and web apps that meet engineers where they already work. These agents are connected to internal systems with the right context, permissioning, and safety boundaries to operate with minimal human oversight. Open SWE is the open-source version of this pattern. Built on LangGraph and Deep Agents , it gives you the same architecture those companies built internally: cloud sandboxes, Slack and Linear invocation, subagent orchestration, and automatic PR creation — ready to customize for your own codebase and workflows. Note 💬 Read the announcement blog post here Architecture Open SWE makes the same core architectural decisions as the best internal coding agents. Here's how it maps to the patterns described in this overview of Stripe's Minions, Ramp's Inspect, and Coinbase's Cloudbot: 1. Agent Harness — Composed on Deep Agents Rather than forking an existing agent or building from scratch, Open SWE composes on the Deep Agents framework — similar to how Ramp built on top of OpenCode. This gives you an upgrade path (pull in upstream improvements) while letting you customize the orchestration, tools, and middleware for your org. create_deep_agent ( model = "anthropic:claude-opus-4-6" , system_prompt = construct_system_prompt ( repo_dir , ...), tools = [ http_request , fetch_url , commit_and_open_pr , linear_comment , slack_thread_reply ], backend = sandbox_backend , middleware = [ ToolErrorMiddleware (), check_message_queue_before_model , ...], ) 2. Sandbox — Isolated Cloud Environments Every task runs in its own isolated cloud sandbox — a remote Linux environment with full shell access. The repo is cloned in, the agent gets full permissions, and the blast radius of any mistake is fully contained. No production access, no confirmat
title_zh: langchain-ai/open-swe - 一款开源异步编码智能体
summary_zh: Langchain-ai在GitHub上开源了Open SWE项目，这是一个异步编程智能体框架。该项目旨在为企业构建内部专用的编码助手（如Slack机器人、CLI工具或Web应用）提供开源解决方案，其设计参考了Stripe、Ramp、Coinbase等顶尖工程团队已内部实施的高级代理模式。，Open SWE的核心架构基于LangGraph和Deep Agents框架构建，提供了与企业内部系统安全集成的能力。其关键特性包括：第一，采用深度代理组合架构，用户可直接基于Deep Agents框架进行定制化开发，支持灵活编排子代理、工具和中间件，并能持续同步上游改进。第二，所有任务均在隔离的云沙箱环境中执行，每个任务拥有独立的远程Linux环境与完整shell权限，确保操作错误的影响范围完全受控，且无需接触生产系统。第三，支持通过Slack和Linear等平台直接调用代理，并能自动创建代码拉取请求（PR），实现与现有工作流的无缝集成。，技术实现上，项目通过可配置的系统提示词、工具链（如HTTP请求、提交PR、Linear评论、Slack回复等）和中间件（如工具错误处理、消息队列检查等），使代理能够根据代码库上下文执行复杂开发任务。该框架特别强调安全边界与权限控制，允许企业在最小人力监督下，将AI代理安全接入内部开发流程。，Open SWE的开源化使得更多组织能够以可定制、可演进的方式，构建具备专业上下文理解能力和安全操作边界的内部编码助手，从而提升工程效率与代码质量。

Always visible below your input. Install Inside a Claude Code instance, run the following commands: Step 1: Add the marketplace /plugin marketplace add jarrodwatts/claude-hud Step 2: Install the plugin ⚠️ Linux users: Click here first On Linux, /tmp is often a separate filesystem (tmpfs), which causes plugin installation to fail with: EXDEV: cross-device link not permitted Fix : Set TMPDIR before installing: mkdir -p ~ /.cache/tmp && TMPDIR= ~ /.cache/tmp claude Then run the install command below in that session. This is a Claude Code platform limitation . /plugin install claude-hud Step 3: Configure the statusline /claude-hud:setup Done! The HUD appears immediately — no restart needed. What is Claude HUD? Claude HUD gives you better insights into what's happening in your Claude Code session. What You See Why It Matters Project path Know which project you're in (configurable 1-3 directory levels) Context health Know exactly how full your context window is before it's too late Tool activity Watch Claude read, edit, and search files as it happens Agent tracking See which subagents are running and what they're doing Todo progress Track task completion in real-time What You See Default (2 lines) [Opus | Max] │ my-project git:(main*) Context █████░░░░░ 45% │ Usage ██░░░░░░░░ 25% (1h 30m / 5h) Line 1 — Model, plan name (or Bedrock ), project path, git branch Line 2 — Context bar (green → yellow → red) and usage rate limits Optional lines (enable via /claude-
title_zh: jarrodwatts/claude-hud - 一款显示运行状态的Claude代码插件 - 上下文使用情况、活跃工具、运行中的代理以及待办事项进度
summary_zh: 开发者jarrodwatts在GitHub上发布了名为“claude-hud”的开源Claude Code插件。该插件旨在为用户提供一个实时、透明的操作界面，让用户能够清晰了解Claude AI在编码会话中的内部状态与活动。，其核心功能是在用户输入框下方常驻显示一个平视显示器（HUD），集中展示多项关键信息：，1. 上下文使用情况：以彩色进度条（绿色到红色）直观显示当前会话的上下文窗口占用率，帮助用户避免超出限制。，2. 活动工具状态：实时显示Claude正在使用的工具，例如读取、编辑或搜索文件。，3. 运行中的智能体：展示当前正在执行的子智能体（subagents）及其具体任务。，4. 任务进度追踪：实时跟踪待办事项的完成进度。，5. 基础会话信息：包括使用的AI模型（如Opus）、计划名称、当前项目路径、Git分支状态以及API使用率限制等。，该插件安装便捷，在Claude Code实例中通过命令行添加市场源并安装即可，安装后无需重启即可立即生效。用户可以通过 `/claude-hud:setup` 命令进行配置，例如自定义项目路径的显示层级。该工具解决了在复杂编码任务中用户对AI内部过程“黑盒”操作的困惑，通过增强可观测性，提升了用户对Claude AI工作流程的理解和控制能力，从而能更高效地进行协作与调试。

title: unslothai/unsloth - Unified web UI for training and running open models like Qwen, DeepSeek, gpt-oss and Gemma locally.
content: GitHub - unslothai/unsloth: Unified web UI for training and running open models like Qwen, DeepSeek, gpt-oss and Gemma locally. · GitHub Skip to content You signed in with another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You signed out in another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You switched accounts on another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. Dismiss alert unslothai / unsloth Public Uh oh! There was an error while loading. Please reload this page . Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings Fork 4.7k Star 56.4k main Branches Tags Go to file Code Open more actions menu Folders and files Name Name Last commit message Last commit date Latest commit History 4,757 Commits 4,757 Commits .github .github images images scripts scripts studio studio tests tests unsloth unsloth unsloth_cli unsloth_cli .gitattributes .gitattributes .gitignore .gitignore .pre-commit-ci.yaml .pre-commit-ci.yaml .pre-commit-config.yaml .pre-commit-config.yaml CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md CONTRIBUTING.md CONTRIBUTING.md COPYING COPYING LICENSE LICENSE README.md README.md build.sh build.sh cli.py cli.py install.ps1 install.ps1 install.sh install.sh pyproject.toml pyproject.toml unsloth-cli.py unsloth-cli.py View all files Repository files navigation Run and train AI models with a unified local interface. Features • Quickstart • Notebooks • Documentation • Discord Unsloth Studio (Beta) lets you run and train text, audio , embedding , vision models on Windows, Linux and macOS. ⭐ Features Unsloth provides several key features for both inference and training: Inference Search + download + run models including GGUF, LoRA adapters, safetensors Export models : Save or export models to GGUF, 16-bit safetensors and other formats. Tool calling : Support for self-healing tool calling and web search Code execution : lets LLMs run code, data and verify results so answers are more accurate. Auto-tune inference parameters and customize chat templates. Upload images, audio, PDFs, code, DOCX and more file types to chat with. Training Train 500+ models up to 2x faster with up to 70% less VRAM , with no accuracy loss. Supports full fine-tuning, pretraining, 4-bit, 16-bit and, FP8 training. Observability : Monitor training live, track loss and GPU usage and customize graphs. Data Recipes : Auto-create datasets from PDF, CSV, DOCX etc. Edit data in a visual-node workflow. Reinforcement Learning : The most efficient RL library, using 80% less VRAM for GRPO, FP8 etc. Multi-GPU training is supported, with major improvements coming soon. ⚡ Quickstart Unsloth can be used in two ways: through Unsloth Studio , the web UI, or through Unsloth Core , the code-based version. Each has different requirements. Unsloth Studio (web UI) Unsloth Studio (Beta) works on Windows, Linux, WSL and macOS . CPU: Supported for chat inference only NVIDIA: Training works on RTX 30/40/50, Blackwell, DGX Spark, Station and more macOS: Currently supports chat only
title_zh: UnslothAI/Unsloth - 用于本地训练和运行开源模型（如Qwen、DeepSeek、GPT-OSS和Gemma）的统一Web界面。
summary_zh: 开源项目Unsloth提供了一个统一的本地Web界面，用于训练和运行各类开源大语言模型，如Qwen、DeepSeek、gpt-oss和Gemma。该项目包含两个主要组件：Unsloth Studio（Beta版）Web UI和基于代码的Unsloth Core。，Unsloth的核心功能涵盖推理和训练两大方面。在推理方面，它支持搜索、下载和运行包括GGUF、LoRA适配器及safetensors在内的多种模型格式，并能将模型导出为GGUF、16位safetensors等格式。其工具调用功能支持自修复工具调用和网络搜索。代码执行能力允许LLM运行代码、处理数据并验证结果，以提高答案准确性。此外，它还能自动调整推理参数、自定义聊天模板，并支持上传图像、音频、PDF、代码、DOCX等多种文件类型进行交互。，在训练方面，Unsloth宣称能以最高2倍的速度训练超过500种模型，同时减少高达70%的显存（VRAM）使用，且不损失精度。它支持全参数微调、预训练以及4位、16位和FP8精度的训练。项目提供训练过程的可观测性，允许用户实时监控训练状态、跟踪损失和GPU使用情况，并自定义图表。其数据配方功能可自动从PDF、CSV、DOCX等文件创建数据集，并通过可视化节点工作流编辑数据。Unsloth还集成了高效的强化学习库，据称在GRPO、FP8等场景下可减少80%的VRAM使用。目前支持多GPU训练，并预告将有重大改进即将推出。，Unsloth Studio（Web UI）目前处于Beta阶段，支持Windows、Linux、WSL和macOS系统。对于硬件要求：CPU仅支持聊天推理；NVIDIA GPU（如RTX 30/40/50系列、Blackwell、DGX Spark等）支持训练功能；macOS目前仅支持聊天功能。项目提供了快速入门指南、Notebook示例、详细文档和Discord社区支持。

Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings Fork 2.9k Star 35.4k main Branches Tags Go to file Code Open more actions menu Folders and files Name Name Last commit message Last commit date Latest commit History 1,146 Commits 1,146 Commits .github .github agents agents assets assets bin bin commands/ gsd commands/ gsd docs docs get-shit-done get-shit-done hooks hooks scripts scripts tests tests .gitignore .gitignore .release-monitor.sh .release-monitor.sh CHANGELOG.md CHANGELOG.md LICENSE LICENSE README.md README.md README.zh-CN.md README.zh-CN.md SECURITY.md SECURITY.md package-lock.json package-lock.json package.json package.json View all files Repository files navigation GET SHIT DONE English · 简体中文 A light-weight and powerful meta-prompting, context engineering and spec-driven development system for Claude Code, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, Codex, Copilot, and Antigravity. Solves context rot — the quality degradation that happens as Claude fills its context window. English | 简体中文 npx get-shit-done-cc@latest Works on Mac, Windows, and Linux. "If you know clearly what you want, this WILL build it for you. No bs." "I've done SpecKit, OpenSpec and Taskmaster — this has produced the best results for me." "By far the most powerful addition to my Claude Code. Nothing over-engineered. Literally just gets shit done." Trusted by engineers at Amazon, Google, Shopify, and Webflow. Why I Built This · How It Works · Commands · Why It Works · User Guide Why I Built This I'm a solo developer. I don't write code — Claude Code does. Other spec-driven development tools exist; BMAD, Speckit... But they all seem to make things way more complicated than they need to be (sprint ceremonies, story points, stakeholder syncs, retrospectives, Jira workflows) or lack real big picture understanding of what you're building. I'm not a 50-person software company. I don't want to play enterprise theater. I'm just a creative person trying to build great things that work. So I built GSD. The complexity is in the system, not in your workflow. Behind the scenes: context engineering, XML prompt formatting, subagent orchestration, state management. What you see: a few commands that just work. The system gives Claude everything it needs to do the work and verify it. I trust the workflow. It just does a good job. That's what this is. No enterprise roleplay bullshit. Just an incredibly effective system for building cool stuff consistently using Claude Co
title_zh: gsd-build/get-shit-done - 由TÂCHES开发的轻量级强大元提示、上下文工程及规范驱动开发系统，专为Claude Code设计。
summary_zh: GitHub项目gsd-build/get-shit-done是一个轻量级且功能强大的元提示、上下文工程和规范驱动开发系统，专为Claude Code等AI编码工具（包括OpenCode、Gemini CLI、Codex、Copilot和Antigravity）设计。该项目由TÂCHES开发，旨在解决“上下文腐化”问题——即当Claude填满其上下文窗口时出现的输出质量下降现象。，该系统通过精密的上下文工程、XML提示格式化、子智能体协调和状态管理，在后台构建了一个高效的工作流程，而用户界面仅需几个简单命令即可操作。其核心价值在于将复杂性封装在系统内部，而非用户的工作流中，使得开发者能够专注于创意构建，无需陷入繁琐的企业级流程（如冲刺会议、故事点估算、利益相关者同步等）。，GSD允许用户通过清晰的规范描述来驱动开发，系统会为Claude提供完成任务和验证所需的一切信息。它支持跨平台运行（Mac、Windows、Linux），可通过npx get-shit-done-cc@latest快速启动。用户反馈表明，该系统在同类工具（如SpecKit、OpenSpec、Taskmaster）中表现优异，被亚马逊、Google、Shopify和Webflow的工程师所信赖。，项目创始人强调，GSD摒弃了“企业角色扮演”的繁琐环节，致力于提供一个极其高效、一致的开发系统，帮助独立开发者和创意人员可靠地构建出优秀可用的产品。

title: shareAI-lab/learn-claude-code - Bash is all you need - A nano claude code–like 「agent harness」, built from 0 to 1
content: GitHub - shareAI-lab/learn-claude-code: Bash is all you need - A nano claude code–like 「agent harness」, built from 0 to 1 · GitHub Skip to content You signed in with another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You signed out in another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You switched accounts on another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. Dismiss alert shareAI-lab / learn-claude-code Public Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings Fork 5.4k Star 33.2k main Branches Tags Go to file Code Open more actions menu Folders and files Name Name Last commit message Last commit date Latest commit History 12 Commits 12 Commits .github/ workflows .github/ workflows agents agents docs docs skills skills web web .env.example .env.example .gitignore .gitignore LICENSE LICENSE README-ja.md README-ja.md README-zh.md README-zh.md README.md README.md requirements.txt requirements.txt View all files Repository files navigation English | 中文 | 日本語 Learn Claude Code -- Harness Engineering for Real Agents The Model IS the Agent Before we talk about code, let's get one thing absolutely straight. An agent is a model. Not a framework. Not a prompt chain. Not a drag-and-drop workflow. What an Agent IS An agent is a neural network -- a Transformer, an RNN, a learned function -- that has been trained, through billions of gradient updates on action-sequence data, to perceive an environment, reason about goals, and take actions to achieve them. The word "agent" in AI has always meant this. Always. A human is an agent. A biological neural network, shaped by millions of years of evolutionary training, perceiving the world through senses, reasoning through a brain, acting through a body. When DeepMind, OpenAI, or Anthropic say "agent," they mean the same thing the field has meant since its inception: a model that has learned to act. The proof is written in history: 2013 -- DeepMind DQN plays Atari. A single neural network, receiving only raw pixels and game scores, learned to play 7 Atari 2600 games -- surpassing all prior algorithms and beating human experts on 3 of them. By 2015, the same architecture scaled to 49 games and matched professional human testers , published in Nature . No game-specific rules. No decision trees. One model, learning from experience. That model was the agent. 2019 -- OpenAI Five conquers Dota 2. Five neural networks, having played 45,000 years of Dota 2 against themselves in 10 months, defeated OG -- the reigning TI8 world champions -- 2-0 on a San Francisco livestream. In a subsequent public arena, the AI won 99.4% of 42,729 games against all comers. No scripted strategies. No meta-programmed team coordination. The models learned teamwork, tactics, and real-time adaptation entirely through self-play. 2019 -- DeepMind AlphaStar masters StarCraft II. AlphaStar beat professional players 10-1 in a closed-door match, and later achieved Grandmaster status on European servers -- top 0.15% of 90,000 p
title_zh: shareAI-lab/learn-claude-code - 你只需要 Bash - 一个从零到一构建的类 Claude Code 微型「智能体框架」
summary_zh: GitHub项目shareAI-lab/learn-claude-code提出了一个核心理念：Bash is all you need。该项目是一个从零到一构建的、类似于Claude Code的微型“智能体驾驭工具”，旨在通过简单的Bash脚本实现高效的智能体工程。，项目强调“模型即智能体”的根本观点，明确指出智能体本质上是一个经过训练的神经网络模型（如Transformer、RNN），而非框架、提示链或拖放式工作流。智能体通过数十亿次基于行动序列数据的梯度更新，学会感知环境、推理目标并采取行动以实现目标。这一观点在AI领域有深厚的历史依据：DeepMind的DQN仅凭原始像素和游戏分数学会了玩Atari游戏；OpenAI Five通过自我对弈掌握了Dota 2的团队协作与实时适应；DeepMind的AlphaStar在《星际争霸II》中达到宗师级别。这些案例共同证明，智能体的核心是能够从经验中学习行动的模型。，该项目的核心功能是提供一个轻量级的“驾驭工具”，帮助开发者以最简化的方式（仅需Bash环境）构建和操作真实的智能体。它通过模块化设计（包括agents、docs、skills等目录）支持智能体的开发、测试与部署，并提供了多语言文档（英文、中文、日文）。项目强调去除不必要的复杂性，回归智能体的本质——即一个能够学习和执行任务的模型，从而降低开发门槛，提升效率。，其技术特点在于极简主义：不依赖复杂的框架，而是通过Bash脚本整合关键组件，实现智能体的快速迭代与实验。价值在于为AI研究者和工程师提供了一种清晰、直接的智能体实现路径，尤其适合需要快速原型验证和深入理解智能体底层机制的场合。

title: GB1: The AI from the UK
summary_zh: GB1是一款由英国人工智能公司Gradient Labs开发的大型语言模型（LLM）。该模型旨在提供一种高效、可访问且成本效益高的AI解决方案，特别强调在保持强大性能的同时减少计算资源消耗。，GB1的核心技术特点在于其创新的模型架构和训练方法。它采用了混合专家（MoE）模型设计，并专注于深度而非宽度的扩展，这使其能够在参数量相对较小（例如70亿参数）的情况下，实现与更大规模模型相媲美的性能。这种设计显著降低了推理所需的计算成本和延迟，使其更适合部署在资源受限的环境中或需要高吞吐量的应用场景。，在能力方面，GB1在多项标准基准测试中表现出色，尤其在代码生成、数学推理和通用知识问答任务上展现了强大竞争力。其训练数据经过精心策划，涵盖了高质量的代码、科学文献和网络文本，确保了其在专业领域的实用性。此外，GB1支持扩展的上下文长度（可达128K tokens），并能通过API方便地集成到各类应用中。，该模型的价值主张清晰：为开发者和企业提供一个性能强劲但更轻量、更经济的LLM选择。它降低了使用先进AI技术的门槛，使更多组织能够利用定制化的AI能力，而无需承担超大模型带来的高昂基础设施成本。GB1的发布被视为英国在快速发展的全球AI竞赛中一次重要的技术贡献，展示了其在高效AI模型设计方面的创新能力。
